---
title: "dns-nameservers order in dhclient.conf matters?"
date: 2014-03-04
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by David_Etcell on 2014-03-04
I don't know if this is expected behavior or what, but I was having trouble getting wan servers to resolve, though IP addresses were working fine (making me lean toward a DNS problem).
 
My dhclient.conf contained the following line:

```
dns-nameservers 127.0.0.1 192.168.1.1
```

however this made a resolv.conf of:

```
root@rincewind:/# cat /etc/resolv.conf
# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 127.0.0.1
root@rincewind:/#

```
for some reason this was solved by reversing the loop and router ip addresses in the dns-nameservers line of dhclient.conf so it is:
```
dns-nameservers 192.168.1.1 127.0.0.1
[COLOR=#222222][FONT=Verdana]
```[/FONT][/COLOR]

the resolv.conf now contains:

```
root@rincewind:/# cat /etc/resolv.conf
# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 192.168.1.1
nameserver 127.0.0.1
root@rincewind:/#

```

I installed from a 14.04 daily build, but all packages are up to date. Either way, I'm posting this here as solved in case anyone else runs into this...

---
